subject: Physics
title: Quantum Optics
description: A micro-lesson that makes Quantum Optics usable.

hook.text: Quantum optics studies light one photon at a time—and single photons behave very strangely.

buildup.text: Send one photon at a double slit. It still creates an interference pattern—as if it went through both slits.

discovery.text: A single photon interferes with itself. It behaves as a wave until the moment it's detected as a particle.

twist.text: Try to watch which slit it goes through and the interference vanishes. Observation kills the pattern.

climax.text: Quantum optics powers quantum encryption, random number generators, and tests of quantum foundations.

punchline.text: One photon, two slits, interference. Don't watch or it stops.

quiz.question: What happens when you observe which slit a single photon passes through?
